Although  they are frequently overlooked, the fascia and soffit boards are essential parts of your roof system. The water that falls into your gutters cascades down the roof and over the fascia, which is also the part that supports your gutters. The soffit is the part behind the fascia that enables proper ventilation of the attic and doesn t let the moisture condense. So, these two units are a critical step in protecting your home against water infiltration, pests, and the rotting of the wood  frame.

Gutter Guards: Stop Clogs Before They Start
5 Reasons for Raindrop  Gutter Guards:
Downspouts & Drainage Extensions That Know Where to Go
Before & After: Here s All the Proof You ll Need to Trust Us
We put up adaptable gutters that work perfectly. These aren t like the standard gutters from big stores that are likely to sag, rust or come apart in heavy weather.
Say goodbye to drips, dribbles, and disaster. Say hello to peace of mind, no matter how hard it pours.

Our unique sloped grid design breaks the flow of water, forcing water into the gutter. Unlike mesh designs which keep almost everything out of the gutter, including water the hole size of the grid prevents large debris from clogging the gutter but allows small debris to sweep through.

With added UV stabilizers, the Raindrop  gutter guards' polypropylene material is made to be suitably resilient. The dark color of the product soaks in heat from the sun during cold months, naturally melting snow and ice to keep water flowing into the gutters year-round.

Raindrop  gutter guards can withstand a lot of damage, even from trees. Where other materials break, our resilient design and material bends but doesn t break. Raindrop  gutter guards carries a 20-year limited warranty.
Where other gutter guards lay flat to handle water, Raindrop  gutter guards are designed to be installed at a pitch, to effectively extend the roof line, helping to keep debris moving off the gutter.

What s the use of gutters andgutter guards if the water simply flows down around your house directly toward the foundation? That s why we add downspouts and drainage extensions to divert water away from your house so your foundation doesn t get damaged from water. We use stable extension pieces that safely move water to where it should go, unlike the little sad slides you find in some mulch. Consider it a complete gutter solution, not just one part of the process.

Even though you may think that gutters aren t that important, they actually help defend your home from water damage. When it rains, gutters and downspouts trap and channel the water so it doesn t reach your roof, foundation, or landscaping. When your gutters are clogged, water may overflow and can cause problems like flooding your basement, rotting wood, or cracking the foundation. Now, is that something you want to face? Definitely not.

Keeping gutters clean isn t just about appearances; it helps you avoid expensive problems later. Besides, keeping your gutters clean helps them keep up with all the rain or snow Mother Nature sends your way.

We aren t your normal gutter cleaner...we also check thegutters downspoutsfor any clogs. It s important because clean gutters won t stop water from backing up in clogged downspouts and spilling elsewhere.When we clean, we look for any issues you may have and provide you our findings in the event we find issues that should be addressed.

Are You Ready to Take Protect Your House?
If your home could talk, it d probably ask for a makeover starting with your fascia and soffit.
If you re fixing your gutters, you may as well choose a package that replaces both soffits and fascia, too. Trust us, your home will thank you.

The fasciais the long, vertical board that runs along the edge of your roof. It s the section of the roof where your gutters are connected. It acts as the boundary between what s on your roof and everything surrounding it.
The soffitis the part of your home s roof that hangs over your head and is under the eaves when you stand by it. It also allows for ventilation in the attic and keeps pests out.
So when they start looking rough? That s your home waving a red flag.

If you fix your gutters and ignore soffit and fascia damage, it s much like putting a Band-Aid on a broken arm. You might have stopped the water from entering your house, but the bigger problem is still lurking somewhere around the corner.
Let our team take it from there to get your roofline back in tip-top shape.Get in touchwith us today.

Our team members are well-versed and will provide you with the best approaches. Whether you're in an older home with wood fascia or a newer build that needs an aluminum update, we ve seen it, fixed it, and made it look good.
And yeah, we might crack a joke or two while we re on the ladder but we take your home seriously.
The best part? Nobody s hiding behind screens, filtering with wire mesh or using fancy gimmicks. Just solid, enclosed gutters that work.
Most people are unaware of their soffit or fascia until it creates a problem, like a leak or uninvited pests and insects.

Fascia and soffit are the essentials that literally hold and make the home look good. Weather, moisture, and pests can cause these parts to rot over a period of time. The effect of the damage may be limited to your property s aesthetics; however, it can still go as far as water infiltration, wood rot, and roofline  instability.
